name: Rebalance the warp tile split to recover active warps

summary.name: Rebalance the warp tile split to recover active warps
summary.hypothesis: The new 2-warp CTA improved runtime by cutting barrier stalls from 21.86% to 8.89% and raising tensor activity from 13.64% to 15.07%, but it also doubled the register occupancy limit from 12 to 24 and dropped active warps to 45.70%. Retuning the CTA and warp tile split so each warp holds less output state while keeping some of the new per-warp reuse should recover latency hiding without fully giving back the reuse win.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Occupancy and latency hiding from register-limited resident warps, now exposed as low sm__warps_active together with elevated long_scoreboard and mio_throttle stalls.
summary.risk: Changing the warp-to-output mapping can easily reintroduce the barrier overhead that the current best run just removed, so a full rollback to the older 4-warp shape is not automatically a win.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if active warps recover materially but runtime does not beat 88.543102 ms, or if barrier stalls climb back into the high-teens or ~20% range.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
